inputPackage.rule: A standardized Wiki datasource package has not been defined yet. Do not consume raw crawled data directly and do not invent field paths in this mapping.

rules: Only bind data that exists in a confirmed Wiki datasource or development-approved mock.
Do not invent category, entry, route, image, badge, count, progress, attribute, table, or rich-text fields.
Use TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE for every source path until the Wiki datasource contract is confirmed.
Use the shared component registry at skill-pack/components.json. Wiki does not maintain a separate component registry.
Use only components allowed by the target region in skill-pack/wiki/templates/wiki.json.
Keep the default Wiki canvas at 1000 x 610.
Keep mobile mapping undefined until mobile Wiki design nodes or explicit implementation constraints are supplied.
Missing optional fields should collapse the related UI element instead of showing fabricated placeholder content.
Breadcrumb labels and target routes must come from datasource or routing context.
Pagination state must come from datasource or renderer state, not from hardcoded sample values.
Home summary title follows the WikiHome two-segment title row: '<game name>' followed by the literal 'Wiki攻略'.
Nav.TopBar items are global first-level Wiki categories.
Form.SearchBar is global Wiki search across categories and entries.
For Game.CategoryCard in home.category-grid, filter out categories whose linked secondary page or category total item count is 0.

For Game.CategoryCard in home.category-grid, avatarSrcs must preview up to the first three items of the linked secondary page in display order, using item icon first or item image when no icon exists.
For Game.CategoryCard in home.category-grid, countLabel must represent the total item count for the linked secondary page or category, not the avatar preview count.
Treat template pages as placement references, not datasource shape requirements. Referencing a template page does not require rendering every observed region or component in that frame.
Select the component family from the original source page structure and information density first, then apply the closest matching template layout for that component family.

For any Wiki component that renders imageSrc, inspect source asset dimensions or visual content and bind imageRatio to the closest supported Base.Image ratio before rendering. Do not blindly use 1:1 for wide images; weapons, equipment thumbnails, maps, screenshots, landscapes, and banners should usually use 3:2 or 16:9.

When a secondary page renders no second-level navigation, no third-level navigation/sidebar, and no breadcrumbs above its list, place the list region 20px from the Body top. Do not inherit the lower list y offset from templates that reserve space for breadcrumbs or secondary navigation.

In secondary.card-list, choose Game.ShowCard only when every entry's subtitle/body text fits within one visible line and the card can fully represent the original source entry; choose compact Layout.DescribeCard when the entry needs richer inline description, image, badge, or meta fields; choose the SecondaryPageLargeCard Layout.DescribeCard grid only when the original source page is image-first or gallery-like.

If any source card in the same secondary, multi-level, or related card page/list needs more than one visible line of secondary/rich text, use Layout.DescribeCard with size=SM for all related cards in that page/list instead of mixing Game.ShowCard with DescribeCard. Bind the subtitle/body text to the SM description node as a two-line region; expose the full string through Base.ToolTip only when the rendered text is actually truncated or hidden.

Using SecondaryPageLargeCard means using its large-card placement rules for chosen Layout.DescribeCard entries, not forcing all source pages into large image cards. When the chosen large-card entries render only one row, including itemCount <= 4 or fewer than four cards, vertically center that row inside the large-card list container.
Use secondary.multi-level-tabs only when the original source page exposes second-level branches under the active first-level Nav.TopBar category.

Use secondary.multi-level-sidebar only when the active secondary tab exposes real third-level groups or local subcategory anchors. If the source page has only second-level navigation, omit the sidebar and do not fabricate third-level items.

Use secondary.multi-level-card-list only when the entries under the active second-level tab and optional active third-level group are best represented by the observed two-column Game.ShowCard list and every entry subtitle/body fits within one visible line. Otherwise keep the matched navigation placement and choose the content component that best fits the original page structure.

For Game.ShowCard, derive targetRoute or hasNextLevel from the source hierarchy. If the represented entry is terminal and has no next page, set hasNextLevel=false or showRightIcon=false so the right arrow is hidden.

For Game.ShowCard descriptions, pass the full subtitle/body text even though the component visibly clamps to one line; the full text must be exposed through Base.ToolTip only when the visible text is actually truncated or hidden, with a short hover delay and auto placement that avoids viewport or scroll-container clipping.
Breadcrumbs are only for final detail pages. Each breadcrumb label is clickable and maps to the route for that hierarchy level.
Detail hero card component family follows the card family used on the previous page for the entry that opened the detail page.
For detail.attribute-section badges, keep one stable tone for each source tag class and use different tones for different classes when source colors are unavailable.
For detail.extended-section, render content only when the original source structure matches supported rich text, media, table, or extra section blocks.
Detail tables must use structured column and row data when available; do not encode table data as plain rich text.

fieldMappings.source: TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > game name composed as '<game name>' + fixed 'Wiki攻略' suffix, visible first-level category count, and total item count
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > top-level categories with total item count greater than 0
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > each visible top-level category > first three linked secondary-page items > icon or image asset URLs in display order, preferring icon when both icon and image exist
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > each visible top-level category > total item count for the linked secondary page or category
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE_OR_ROUTING_CONTEXT > each visible top-level category > linked secondary-page route
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE_OR_ROUTING_CONTEXT > global first-level Wiki categories and active first-level category

TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > selected category entries plus routing hierarchy plus per-entry image asset dimensions/content type, choosing one card family for the page/list: Game.ShowCard only when all entry subtitle/body text fits one visible line, compact Layout.DescribeCard size=SM when any entry needs more than one visible line or richer inline fields, or SecondaryPageLargeCard-style Layout.DescribeCard when the original source structure is image-first
fieldMappings.target: regions[secondary.card-list].components[Game.ShowCard or Layout.DescribeCard].dataBinding.entries, entries[].imageRatio, selected card family, selected layout variant, and Layout.DescribeCard size=SM when replacing ShowCard for richer text

fieldMappings.source: TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE_OR_RENDERER_STATE > pagination current page from renderer or route, totalPages from backend
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE_OR_ROUTING_CONTEXT > active first-level category > second-level navigation branches
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE_OR_ROUTING_CONTEXT > active second-level branch > third-level groups or local anchors plus active third-level id

TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > entries filtered by active first-level category, active second-level tab, optional active third-level group, per-entry next-level route availability, and page/list-level check that every entry subtitle/body fits one visible line before using Game.ShowCard
fieldMappings.target: regions[secondary.multi-level-card-list].components[Game.ShowCard or Layout.DescribeCard].dataBinding.entries and selected card family

fieldMappings.source: TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE_OR_ROUTING_CONTEXT > final detail page hierarchy labels and target routes
RENDERER_SCROLL_STATE > real detail page scroll position, scroll height, viewport height, and source/template scrollbar placement
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE_OR_ROUTING_CONTEXT > selected detail summary and previous-page card family
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > selected detail attributes, source tag classes, source colors when available, and badge labels
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > related entries and progress values
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > structured detail sections and tables
TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE > extended rich text, media, additional tables, or extra sections that match supported detail blocks
